title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves. The bond carries a 3.5% annual coupon and matures on 18 February 2029. This represents the first operation under the Bank's updated issuance programme announced in September 2025, which shifted to two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and enhanced transparency.
key_points: Bank of England launched a US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond on 10 February 2026
Bond pays a coupon of 3.5% per annum with maturity on 18 February 2029
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
In September 2025, the BoE announced it would shift to targeting two benchmark issuances annually
The updated issuance programme features a regular timetable and high degree of transparency
BoE foreign exchange reserves are separate from the Government's own foreign exchange reserves
why_it_matters: The Bank of England's structured approach to financing foreign currency reserves through two annual benchmark issuances with a regular timetable provides market participants with predictable access to BoE reserve debt. The composition of Joint Lead Managers (RBC, BMO, HSBC, Morgan Stanley) indicates international distribution capability for this UK sovereign issuer.
macro_context: The issuance relates to the Bank of England's foreign currency reserve management, which supports monetary policy and financial stability objectives. The shift to a structured, semi-annual issuance schedule (announced September 2025) reflects a move toward greater operational transparency in reserve financing.
market_structure_context: The issuance follows a RegS/144A format, indicating compliance with US securities regulations for international bond distribution. The Joint Lead Manager syndicate structure (four banks) is typical for benchmark-sized sovereign issuances, providing diversified distribution across US and international markets.

issues_found: Minor date ambiguity: metadata shows page published 27 March 2026, while source body states event date of 10 February 2026 and was 'previously announced on 9 February 2026' – this is not an error in the record, merely reflects the nature of Bank of England website updates

notes: This is a market notice documenting a specific bond issuance operation. The substantive content is relatively thin—the majority of text is legal disclaimers, jurisdiction restrictions, and forward-looking statement notices. Key factual content includes the issuance size (US$2.5bn), maturity (3-year), coupon (3.5%), maturity date (18 February 2029), and Joint Lead Manager syndicate. The page also references the September 2025 policy change to two annual benchmark issuances, providing structural context. Confidence lowered due to limited original analytical content and heavy reliance on boilerplate language.
